Plaintiffs Adam O'Neill And Maria O'Neill'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs' Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Requests For Production Of Documents, And Requests For Admission, And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-07-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
James Driscoll, Esq. (415-673-6000) for Plaintiff/ opposing party Adam O'Neill.
Anne Gritzer, Esq. (415-544-7142) for Defendant/Moving party Pierre Berterretche, Marie Berterretche, Dominic Berterretche, And Marie Elissetche.

9:32 am. Parties are ordered to meet and confer in the hallway. Defendant is only contesting one additional response. Plaintiff would argue sanctions.

10:13 am. The matter is recalled. ARGUED. Plaintiff indicates some requests were withdrawn. Defendant argues it has prepared discovery responses except for #15 due to privacy issues, over-reach, not necessary. Plaintiff argues landlord, time of 30 days - too long, sanctions.
Plaintiff requests 15 days, Defendant concurs. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules adopts the tentative ruling with modification as follows:

Plaintiffs Adam O'Neill And Maria O'Neill'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs' Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Requests For Production Of Documents, And Requests For Admission, And Request For Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED as follows: defendant shall within 15 days serve substantive responses to: Special Interrogatories 6, 7, 10, 11, 13, 14, 15, 19, 20, 21, 34, 35; and Form Interrogatories 12.4, 12.5, 13.1, and 13.2. Defendants shall provide a further substantive response to Request for Admission 3, 9, 11, and 13. Defendants shall within 30 days serve a further response to plaintiffs' Requests for Production of Documents responsive to Requests 5, 9, 11, 12, 13, 17, 19, 23, 24, 25, 26, 27, 29, 35, 36, 39, 41, and 42. Defendants shall state that they do not have and never have had, or if they have, why they no longer have requested documents. "Not Applicable" or "N/A" is not responsive. Defendants shall serve all responsive documents it has within 30 days. Defendant's Preliminary Statement and General Objections are ordered stricken as to all interrogatories and document demands. (See Rutter Guide, Civ. Pro. Before Trial at sec. 8:1071. "Objections: In lieu of answering or allowing inspection of records, above, the responding party may serve objections. Each objection must be stated separately (no objections to entire set), and must bear the same number or letter as the interrogatory to which it is directed. [CCP 2030.210(a)(3)] Objections must be specific. A motion to compel lies where objections are "too general." [CCP 2030.300(a)(3); see Korea Data Systems Co. Ltd. v. Sup.Ct. (Amazing Technologies Corp.) (1997) 51 CA4th 1513, 1516, 59 CR2d 925, 926-objecting party subject to sanctions for "boilerplate" objections." The same rule applies to objections to document demands- see CCP 2031.210.) Special Interrogatory 15 is taken under submission. Sanctions is taken under submission. Plaintiff submits proposed orders. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)
